config_notes: Runbook config requested track=light, but Solo Orchestrator init.sh enforces a Production-Build governance constraint that rejects 'light' (Production requires standard or full). Test was completed with track=standard as the closest valid track for the scenario. mcp_server platform appears as 'mcp-server' in init.sh (hyphenated). Heredoc-driven init was killed on the Git host prompt; manual fake remote was added per runbook constraint.

tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh hung on the 'Git host:' prompt after the heredoc was exhausted; killed at 120s. Project skeleton WAS produced before the hang (CLAUDE.md, PROJECT_INTAKE.md, .claude/, workflows, etc.). Post-init wiring (host repo creation, branch protection, phase2_init markings 2-4) had to be performed manually.

tests.details: .github/workflows/release.yml present (platform=mcp-server).

tests.details: .claude/framework directory present (CDF v4.2.3, profile=web-api, 14 rules, 15 hooks).

tests.details: verify-install.sh reports 'Context7 MCP installed' and 'Context7 MCP configured'.

tests.details: docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md present; only mcp-server.md (no extras) — UAT-platform reference also copied.

tests.name: no_residual_backup_dir
tests.details: .claude-backup/ removed by init.sh ('Removed CDF migration backup' log line confirms BUG-002 regression check).

tests.name: pre_commit_gate_registered
tests.details: .claude/settings.json PreToolUse Bash hooks include 'bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh'.

tests.name: stop_checklist_registered
tests.details: .claude/settings.json Stop hooks include $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/framework/hooks/stop-checklist.sh.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers into bash init.sh; the prompt order shifts based on conditional branches (e.g., light+production triggers an extra track re-prompt; Git host + visibility prompts appear AFTER project creation when no intake-progress.json exists).
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh accepts --non-interactive plus structured flags (--project, --platform, --track, --deployment, --governance, --language, --dir, --git-host, --visibility) so UAT and CI can drive deterministically.
framework_bugs.actual: Only --dry-run and --help flags exist. Heredoc driver silently mis-aligns when conditional re-prompts fire; init.sh hangs on the post-creation Git host prompt once the heredoc is exhausted, leaving phase2_init steps 2-4 unmarked even though the project itself is usable.

framework_bugs.summary: check-phase-gate.sh emits 'integer expression expected' for organizational deployments
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash scripts/check-phase-gate.sh in a fresh organizational/Production project with current_phase=0.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh: line 204: [: 0\n0: integer expression expected. The grep -c | wc-style chained pipeline returns a multi-line count when APPROVAL_LOG.md has the expected sectioning, breaking the [ -lt 6 ] integer test.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook says platform identifier is 'mcp_server' but the actual filename / init.sh prompt label is 'mcp-server' (hyphen). Either rename platform module to mcp_server.md or document the substitution.

Production-Build governance silently up-converts track=light to standard/full but the 'config' table in the runbook treats light/standard/full as freely combinable with any scenario. Either pre-validate the matrix and drop production+light as a config, or document the up-convert.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh:328-378 (TRACK re-prompt under organizational + non-private POC)

documentation_gaps.gap: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 is a real action but is missing from --help output.

No script advances .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase from 0→1, 1→2, 2→3, or 3→4. The runbook test 'phase_state_advances_to_2' is described as 'after gate passes and approval is logged' but check-phase-gate.sh only consistency-checks; advancement is implicit/manual.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh + docs/reference/builders-guide.md (no documented procedure to flip current_phase)

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 → RC=0; phase1_architecture object created in process-state.json.

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: jq '.phase1_architecture.steps_completed' returns [].

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) accepted via --complete-step in order.

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh prints 'Phase gates consistent.' RC=0 (despite cosmetic shell-arithmetic warning noted as Phase 0 bug).

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: No script advances current_phase. Updated phase-state.json manually (current_phase=2, gates.phase_0_to_1 + phase_1_to_2 dated) so the rest of the test could proceed. See Phase 0 documentation gap.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-feature 'uat-feat-1' set .build_loop.feature='uat-feat-1' and started_at.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 5 build_loop steps + feature_recorded accepted; security_audit artifact gate enforced (forced creation of docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md).

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded with all 5 steps complete; .git/hooks/pre-commit (gitleaks + Semgrep + TDD) ran and passed.

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: BL-006 ENFORCEMENT GAP: After feature_recorded completes, .build_loop.feature stays set to the prior feature name and all 5 steps remain marked complete. A subsequent feat: commit without --start-feature is therefore ALLOWED by both the shell git pre-commit path and the PreToolUse pre-commit-gate.sh path. require_build_loop_state_for_commit only blocks when feature is null OR a step is incomplete; neither is true after a successful loop. Reproduced: 'git commit --allow-empty -m "feat(bl006-trigger): no loop"' returned RC=0; PreToolUse hook returned RC=0 with no deny output.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer wrote .claude/pending-approval.json; PreToolUse pre-commit-gate.sh emitted permissionDecision=deny with the pending question + remediation.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel; subsequent gate invocation returned RC=0 with no output (allow).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: jq '.features_completed' .claude/build-progress.json returns ['uat-feat-1'].

framework_bugs.summary: BL-006 fails to block feat: commits after a completed Build Loop
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) Complete a Build Loop end-to-end including feature_recorded. 2) Without calling --start-feature for a new feature, run: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(other): no loop'. The commit is permitted; the PreToolUse pre-commit-gate.sh path also returns RC=0 with no deny.
framework_bugs.expected: After feature_recorded completes, the prior loop's state should not satisfy require_build_loop_state_for_commit for a NEW feat: commit. Either reset .build_loop.feature to null on feature_recorded completion, or compare the commit message's feat scope to the active feature, or require feature_recorded to clear steps_completed.
framework_bugs.actual: process-checklist.sh:require_build_loop_state_for_commit returns 0 because feature != null and all 5 required steps are still marked complete from the prior loop. Result: a single completed loop unlocks unlimited subsequent feat: commits with no Build Loop work.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook 'each_step_completes_in_order' assumes process-checklist sequential enforcement is documented; phase2_init steps 2-4 (branch_protection_configured, project_scaffolded, data_model_applied) have no auto-marking outside init.sh's host-repo flow, so a partial init leaves the project unable to commit without manual --complete-step calls.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase3 ran phase 2→3 bug gate (no open SEV-1/2/3) and warned 'feature(s) since last UAT session'; RC=0.

tests.name: start_uat_creates_session
tests.details: --start-uat 1 set .uat_session.session_id=1 and started_at.

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated HTML file argument and prints usage when called with no args (RC=0). No 'generated UAT scenario' was actually emitted by --start-uat — the runbook assertion has no canonical artifact to lint against. Marking partial: lint script exists and prints clean usage but is not exercised end-to-end.

tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched through gate_passed) accepted via --complete-step.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: All 9 PHASE3_STEPS accepted; no failure on legal_review (added 'Attorney / Legal Review — N/A' entry to APPROVAL_LOG.md to satisfy the conditional check).

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated HTML file but neither --start-uat nor any other script generates one; the runbook 'uat_template_lints_clean' assertion has no source artifact path documented.

Phase 3 'legal_review' artifact check warns when no PRIVACY_POLICY.md / TERMS_OF_SERVICE.md exist but offers no clean N/A path for server-side mcp-server projects without user data — work-around required (manual APPROVAL_LOG entry).

tests.details: monitoring_configured BLOCKED with 'HANDOFF.md not found' until HANDOFF.md was created with a Section 8 monitoring entry. After creation the step succeeded.

tests.name: release_notes_required

tests.name: phase4_completion_state

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep -liE 'password|api[_-]?key|secret|token' returned only framework documentation/templates/CI workflow files that legitimately reference these tokens (no values). No secrets in committed payload.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log does not exist; no FORCE/reset events occurred during the test run (matches runbook expectation).

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returned nothing.

summary: Production / mcp-server / typescript walk-through completes Phases 0-4 but uncovers one Critical BL-006 enforcement gap (completed Build Loops permanently unlock subsequent feat: commits) plus the expected init.sh non-scriptability High finding; init.sh succeeds at producing the project skeleton but hangs on the post-creation Git host prompt when driven by heredoc.
